content.body: Why does the PR preview fail for https://github.com/whatwg/fullscreen/pull/232 ? I don't get the build error when building locally (and I added "removing steps" to the link-defaults)
https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12395 also fails, but with some sort of internal error
oh it succeeds now, seems intermittent

https://t.me/BlackHatNexus

RE language tags in the spellcheck dictionary proposal https://github.com/whatwg/html/issues/12388. It was discussed, https://github.com/Igalia/explainers/issues/44, but we dropped it from the proposal and it's not in the explainer. TAG feedback did not include it as far as I know.

would anyone be opposed to linking the stages document from https://github.com/whatwg/html ? It feels to me like it should be linked there or sort of 1 click removed at least, but I don't know where that would be

I think it would make sense for the README.md to mention WHATWG (e.g. "... the HTML Standard, one of many web standards developed at WHATWG"), and have that link to https://whatwg.org/. From there, the stages doc is 2 clicks away. (It's also 2 clicks away from the "Code of Conduct", which is already linked from README; however, if someone were looking for the stages doc, I wouldn't expect them to click on a "Code of Conduct" link.)
Luke Warlow: I wonder if we should remove styles from css-forms-1 that are in HTML as https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/pull/13778 didn't make it into HTML until today for instance.
